label: Codex App Runtime
notes: This manifest models Codex app/runtime capabilities that are now part of the baseline platform surface but not yet all represented as baseline-owned deep contracts.
The manifest does not change Codex CLI orchestration authority; it records the expanded app/runtime surface so later contracts can land under governance instead of consumer-local heuristics.

surfaces.label: Continuity
notes: Thread reuse, automation continuity, and memory should integrate with workflow and governance instead of bypassing baseline state.

surfaces.label: Desktop shell
notes: Primary desktop/app surface where operator-visible Codex capabilities now converge.

surfaces.label: Extensibility
notes: Plugin and review surfaces should be discoverable as app/runtime capabilities before deeper integration contracts land.

surfaces.label: Remote workspace
notes: Remote workspace access must remain governed by explicit workspace identity and trust posture contracts.

capabilities.label: In-app browser
capabilities.summary: Codex app now includes a browser surface that should be represented as a governed baseline capability instead of opaque external state.
authorities: Admit browser interaction as a governed runtime capability.
constraints: Do not treat browser execution as an unmanaged side effect.
notes: WEB-01 will add deeper contracts and replay-grade artifacts for browser sessions and page annotations.

capabilities.label: Computer use
capabilities.summary: Codex app supports computer-use style interaction that baseline must later represent through trace and policy contracts.
authorities: Admit desktop/computer-use interaction as governed runtime posture.
constraints: Desktop interaction must remain policy-aware and auditable.
notes: WEB-01 will carry the deep trace/audit surface for this capability.

capabilities.label: File preview
capabilities.summary: Codex app can preview files and richer local artifacts directly from the app surface.
authorities: Expose file preview as an app/runtime capability so consumers can distinguish preview support from baseline-owned artifact contracts.
constraints: Baseline should not encode product-specific preview UI behavior.
notes: Preview support is app-native, but baseline should still advertise it as part of the runtime surface.

capabilities.label: Image generation
capabilities.summary: Codex app can generate images and should expose prompt/run/result provenance through governed baseline artifacts.
authorities: Treat image generation as a first-class baseline workflow surface rather than an ad hoc side tool.
constraints: Keep provenance, policy, and consumer-sync posture explicit.
notes: IMG-01 will introduce the shared contract family for image-generation workflow artifacts.

capabilities.label: App memory
capabilities.summary: Codex app now exposes memory and preference continuity signals that should feed governed baseline memory rather than uncontrolled writeback.
authorities: Admit app memory as governed input to baseline memory and workflow systems.
notes: MEM-05 will turn app memory into governed baseline memory candidates and summaries.

capabilities.label: Multiple terminals
capabilities.summary: Codex app supports multiple terminal contexts and richer terminal orchestration than the current Codex CLI-only baseline model.
authorities: Represent terminal multiplicity as explicit runtime posture rather than implicit shell state.
constraints: Do not create per-agent shell forks in baseline.
notes: This capability is admitted in APP-01 so consumers stop assuming a single terminal context is the only supported posture.

capabilities.label: Plugin inventory
capabilities.summary: Codex app exposes a broader plugin/app integration inventory than the baseline currently models.
authorities: Expose live plugin/app capability posture through baseline-owned catalogs and routing hints.
constraints: Do not land provider-specific plugin behavior in baseline.
notes: PLG-01 will ingest the live app/plugin inventory into governed baseline surfaces.

capabilities.label: Review comments
capabilities.summary: Codex app can work directly with review-comment style surfaces exposed through developer workflows.
authorities: Model review-comment capability as app/runtime posture without embedding GitHub-specific heuristics into baseline.
constraints: Product-specific review culture or provider-specific semantics remain out of baseline.
notes: This capability is intentionally inventory-level in APP-01; deeper reusable review-thread contracts can land later without forcing local consumer heuristics first.

capabilities.label: SSH and devboxes
capabilities.summary: Codex app can operate against SSH/devbox style remote workspaces that baseline should model explicitly.
authorities: Represent remote workspace identity, trust, and provenance through contracts instead of ambient shell context.
constraints: Remote execution must remain bounded by explicit trust posture and workspace identity.
notes: SSH-01 will add the deep remote-workspace contract and artifact lineage.

capabilities.label: Summary pane
capabilities.summary: Codex app exposes richer summary-side surfaces that can complement baseline shell and replay summaries.
authorities: Consumers should treat summary-pane presence as an app/runtime capability and not as justification to duplicate baseline summaries locally.
constraints: Baseline remains the source of truth for governed operator summaries.
notes: APP-01 records summary-pane posture so future shell surfacing can map onto it intentionally.

capabilities.label: Thread automations
capabilities.summary: Codex app can reuse threads and automation context over time; baseline must bridge that into workflow/runtime state.
authorities: Bridge app automations into baseline workflow and resume posture instead of treating them as unmanaged external continuity.
constraints: Automation continuity must not bypass approval and workflow gates.
notes: AUT-01 will connect thread reuse to baseline workflow/runtime artifacts.
